item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I’m late to this party, but dang, this was a good read. Honestly, it had me at “King’s Hawaiian”. I feel like there’s a King (James) Hawaiian bible joke in here somewhere, but I can’t be bothered to make it. +1

item.title: Bready! <3
item.content:encoded:
That's the best SCP I know, at least in my opinion, it's just great!
I'd like to see more articles like this
Good job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: www.reddit.com/r/breadstapledtotrees

When this article was first featured, I think it only had about 135 upvotes.

This article is going down in history my dudes

item.title: Small Consideration
item.content:encoded: When you're outlining the process of creating the skip, you put "verse Mark 14:22-24." You might be better served wording it something like "the book of Mark, chapter 14, verses 22-24" or just Mark 14:22-24". Book, chapter, and verse are implicit in the formatting you have already. Just a bit of pedantry from a former Catholic. Smashing article besides!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am so mad about this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. My only qualm here is with the MTF. If they are stationed there, so they aren't Mobile at all, and if they are meant to contain this anomaly, and are even named after it, how did they were called upon when the anomaly first manifested? They existed before their reason to exist?

But otherwise it was a very good article, and I actually liked -1 as a character, and more than similar skips from older days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After reading this article, I have come to a startling revelation. The SCP wiki was put on this Earth so that it could one day host this SCP! Some may say this site exist to host creepypasta that's written in a similar format, but I say neigh! This site, no the entire internet, is for us to spread the word of our Lord and Savior Jesus Crust to the world!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. It’s mostly hilarious, but just a little unsettling, and above all it’s wonderfully weird. I remember wondering as a very little kid if Jesus was made of bread…

item.title: Or...
item.content:encoded: Or a clone…

item.title: heres a proposal
item.content:encoded: hey mate loved this scp howabout adding a new scp or something that wont shut up about jesus crust?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this one! Not a big serious Grimdark SCP. Just something kinda weird that the foundation wants to keep under wraps. One of those SCPs where it's not world ending if it breaks containment, just kinda annoying.

I like that he isn't ever hostile, and cooperates with the foundation. It makes me wonder why they don't get 3305-1 contained on site. It could be a useful supplement of something like 500.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably a random D class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: who's the virgin they used for the sacrifice though…

item.title: A year late but...
item.content:encoded: The "I much prefer Pepperidge Farm." bit at the end is actually what really sold me on what was an otherwise decently solid SCP. Yes I laughed, but I laughed because it was such a perfectly delivered punch line to end what was already a rather tounge in cheek SCP.

So, like tomorrow is the one-year anniversary of this article, so it's like really fitting that it hit 200 about now. I'm still stunned. I never expected this article to hit 100 when I first posted it.

I'm glad you all have enjoyed my grainest achievement!

vivé le bread puns

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 30.48 cm converts to 12 inches, which is a standard size for a loaf of sliced bread from an American grocery store. I think the author is going for a slight humorous touch, with the in-universe author of the report sticking to a Foundation standard of metric units even when it looks silly because of the conversion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just made a account and this is the first thing I saw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm happy it's not.

item.title: r/breadstapledtotrees
item.content:encoded: r/breadstapledtotrees

item.title: Toast Busters
item.content:encoded: Gamma-84 theme song time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm are they still an mtf if they are not mobile?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the god of bread, what more can I ask? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a fantastic idea, but some stuff takes me out of the article. Namely, the vaguely awkward dialogue in the last addendum (although I love all the bread puns), and the significant digits in the first addendum. Approximately 30.48 centimeters? There's nothing approximate about going to 4 significant digits in a measurement of the length of a loaf of bread. I feel like this is mentioned in one of the writing guides?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alternate potentially offensive takes on religion(s) are pretty common here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is this a featured article? The jokes aren't particularly funny and it's not really clever at all, just "let's take the stupidest interpretation of Christianity possible and make a bunch of lame puns about it", and it's honestly pretty offensive.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought this would be related to the toaster. I'm glad it wasn't because this is so much better.

Caught a mistake, though.

Move the virgin into the center of the center the pentagram.

Also, shouldn't this be tagged "religious"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doughlights me.

item.title: Re: And May You Rise In The Presence Of The Baker
item.content:encoded:
“Do you have a moment to talk about our Lord and Savior, Jesus Crust?”

I've been trying to come up with a way to deliver a similar joke for a while now, except in my version it's "Cheesus Crust", a pizza-based messiah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, for I have burnt it to the ground in praise of our Lord and Grainior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the -J series mean nothing anymore?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I so highly approve of this crosslink. Bread Jesus has needed a tie to Chicken Fried Jesus for so long. (And the cheese one works well enough to add as well)

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Heh. This is an all-around fantastic article, and for the most part is rather standalone, which makes coming up with potential crosslinks difficult. Two potential links could be made to other food-religion articles such as the one where Colonel Sanders is Jesus in SCP-3250 or the one with cheese fanatics attempting to spread their infection in SCP-2867 As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

All individuals who consume part of SCP-3305-1 worship it, as well as bread in general. The form of this worship varies highly between individuals.

To this:

All individuals who consume part of SCP-3305-1 worship it, as well as bread in general. The form of this worship varies highly between individuals. Similarities between this effect and those observed in SCP-3250 and SCP-2867 are under investigation.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
You didn't rise to the occasion very well, but at yeast you're trying.
Seriously well done, you deserve it!

OMG OMG OMG OMG

Some how… the baker has decided to let this article rise in his glory.

But seriously thank you guys. This is my first article to breach 100, so like… its really cool. I'm happy you enjoyed it.

Celebratory Bread Puns
As a toast

I see you're very ryeled up about this.

I hope this skip is engrained in your memory.

I'm sorry if this skip made you feel sourdough.

Butter chance next time!

I loaf you all!

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Thank you for this! It’s everything I imagined and then some.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I don't think this needs to be a -J; there are plenty of skips on the mainlist as funny or funnier than this but they aren't -Js. Anyway, here's wonderwall…

https://youtu.be/wytMzmJAh0g

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for catching that! Honestly, its surprising how many typos like that people are able to just gloss over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Found a typo and fixed it for you :)
"I have returned to once again to enlighten men and women"
Changed to "I have returned once again to enlighten men and women"

Also +1 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well it made me break out into an enormous grin at "and puncture itself to produce wine to drink" so I'm definitely up-voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never thought that a non-J skip with a description so short could be so great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would have loved for the buoyancy line to have made it in, but still, this is a good skip. I like that Bread Jesus wasn't just a monster and hated what his followers did.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: r/BreadStapledToTrees is happy that they've been noticed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Normally religion focused scps aren't a good read, but I think you did quite well for a third scp. Also puns. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whatever else can be said about it, I don't think it necessarily needs to be a J. It's silly, yes, but it's played completely straight in-universe.

That said, the last line secured an upvote from me. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, for one, accept our new bread overlord.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the tag. Please wait for the technical team to tag articles if you're not going to add every relevant tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed that the author forgot to add tags to the article, so I added "scp" tag to make it properly show in "top rated new pages".

Sorry if I offended the author by editing this article..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not directly, but I should have. I do not condone their message. Wonderbread is terribly bland. I much prefer Pepperidge Farm.

…eh. This seems extremely out of place in a regular skip, and I feel like its status as one has limited its potential. It's stuck in this awkward place where it isn't fully committed to being serious or to being funny. Making it a -J would improve it quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
stabbed with to death

small error you should fix

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really like this. It's original, kinda creepy, but really humorous, something very rare on the site.

item.title: wut
item.content:encoded: wut

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1.

The SCP was average, but the addendums were the high points of the article, and were very funny.

I only have 1 complaint.

I don't think the foundation would include the ritual in the article itself, it seems too risky to do that, in case someone tries to do the ritual.

EDIT: I just searched up Mark 14:22-24, and that just made this so much better.

+ Read it yourself
- Right Here

22 While they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke it and gave it to his disciples, saying, “Take it; this is my body.”

23 Then he took a cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them, and they all drank from it.

24 “This is my blood of the[a] covenant, which is poured out for many,” he said to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I wanted to downvote this thing so bad. But I just couldn't when I read this line:

I’m just a humble butter knife, spreading the words of The Baker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isaiah 55:2 "Why do you give your money for that which is not bread, and your labor for what does not satisfy?"

item.title: ONE LOAF TO RULE THEM ALL
item.content:encoded: BREAD FOR THE BREAD LORD ON HIS BREAD THRONE IN THE LAND OF BREADDOR WHERE THE BREAD LIES!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WAIT, this was all to set up a joke about PEPPERIDGE FARM?!?!

ARE YOU KIDDING ME?!

In all seriousness, this reminds me of a joke from a Map Game on Althistory wiki involving a pancake and waffle god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand. I'm considering changing it to a -J, but I made a thread proposing this idea. Depending on the responses to the proposal I might delete this and reupload it as 3305-J (or whatever logistical process this would need to go through).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would have been a great -J
